title: Doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't
post:
This isn't a comfortable message, but it's the truth as I see it: the gap between AI-enabled doctors and non-AI-enabled doctors is going to widen. Fast.

A doctor who uses AI for differential diagnosis, treatment planning, and staying current with literature will simply provide better care than one who doesn't. That's not an opinion — it's an arithmetic result. More data processed, more patterns recognized, more possibilities considered.

The irony is that many medical professionals see AI as a threat to their profession. The ones who embrace it will be the ones who thrive in it.

What do you think is holding healthcare professionals back from AI adoption? Thoughts?
